item.name: Jeep Cherokee whitepaper-derived function-level attack scenarios (implemented set, pipeline-safe)
item.description: Function-level threat scenarios reconstructed from the Miller & Valasek Jeep Cherokee/Uconnect whitepaper and aligned to the current emulator and pipeline_v4 inputs. The scenario set is preserved, while schema and wording are adjusted for safer threat-layer use and better pipeline compatibility.

method_note.source_basis: Charlie Miller and Chris Valasek, Remote Exploitation of an Unaltered Passenger Vehicle (Black Hat USA / IOActive, 2015).
method_note.construction_policy: Each scenario is expressed as an attack objective + entry point + endpoint combination. Each attack-tree step is written as a natural-language one-operation statement at the functional level rather than as an exact API name, port number, or arbitration ID.
method_note.defenseweaver_alignment: The scenarios are organized in a DefenseWeaver-style structure: attack objective, entry point, endpoint, logical path summary, and function-level attack methods. Concrete execution values are intended to be grounded later through system_model/testbed binding rather than embedded directly in the threat text.
method_note.pipeline_compatibility_note: This file uses the generalized-tree schema with attack_tree.root/nodes plus explicit attack_paths so that the uploaded pipeline_v4 code can preserve path order, path-level control context, and downstream evidence labels.
method_note.binding_boundary_note: Threat-layer text intentionally avoids exact implementation bindings in titles and attack-step descriptions. Connection references (CONN_x) are retained only as abstract links to downstream grounding stages.
method_note.selection_policy: All scenarios with emulator_execution=implemented are retained, including direct whitepaper scenarios, surrogate emulator realizations, and emulator-supported extension scenarios.
method_note.count_note: 19 scenarios total: TS_J02, TS_J03, TS_J04, TS_J05, TS_J06, TS_J07, TS_J09, TS_J10, TS_J11, TS_J12, TS_J13, TS_J14, TS_J15, TS_J16, TS_J17, TS_J18, TS_J19, TS_J20, TS_J21.
method_note.dual_tagging_note: emulator_execution indicates runtime availability in the current emulator. whitepaper_fidelity distinguishes direct whitepaper chains, surrogate emulator realizations, and emulator-specific extensions.

damage_scenarios.description: Remote-exposure and session-establishment scenarios derived from the Jeep Cherokee whitepaper

threat_scenarios.title: Remote attacker confirms the IVI control service is externally reachable
threat_scenarios.logical_path_summary: Externally reachable IVI network interface -> IVI remote-control service
threat_scenarios.threat_scenario: A remote attacker reaches the IVI control service from an external network and confirms that the service is not restricted to internal interfaces.
threat_scenarios.execution_support_note: The emulator exposes the IVI control service over TCP and can be configured to reject or accept external access with the port-restriction flag.
threat_scenarios.cybersecurity_goal: The IVI control service must not be reachable from external networks.
threat_scenarios.precondition: An IVI network interface is exposed to an external network.

T1.description: The attacker identifies the target IVI network address.

T2.description: The attacker attempts to connect to the externally exposed IVI control service.

T3.description: The attacker verifies whether the external connection is accepted or rejected.

attack_paths.path_description: Remote attacker confirms the IVI control service is externally reachable

threat_scenarios.title: A remote attacker establishes an unauthenticated IVI control session
threat_scenarios.entry_point: Externally exposed IVI remote-control entry point
threat_scenarios.logical_path_summary: Externally exposed IVI remote-control entry point -> unauthenticated IVI control session
threat_scenarios.threat_scenario: A remote attacker connects to the exposed IVI control entry point and establishes a control session without presenting valid authentication.
threat_scenarios.execution_support_note: The emulator reproduces D-Bus-over-TCP session establishment and can toggle anonymous authentication acceptance.
threat_scenarios.cybersecurity_goal: The externally exposed IVI control service shall not allow unauthenticated session establishment.
threat_scenarios.precondition: An externally reachable IVI remote-control entry point exists.

T1.description: The attacker connects to the exposed IVI remote-control entry point.

T2.description: The attacker starts the remote control session negotiation.

T3.description: The attacker verifies whether unauthenticated session establishment is permitted.

T4.description: The attacker reaches a state in which control messages can be exchanged.

attack_paths.path_description: A remote attacker establishes an unauthenticated IVI control session

threat_scenarios.title: A remote attacker enumerates the exposed IVI service surface
threat_scenarios.attack_vector: Remote network via IVI control session
threat_scenarios.endpoint: Inventory of exposed services, interfaces, and callable functions
threat_scenarios.logical_path_summary: Unauthenticated IVI control session -> inventory of exposed services, interfaces, and callable functions
threat_scenarios.threat_scenario: A remote attacker uses an unauthenticated IVI control session to enumerate exposed services, interfaces, and callable functions on the IVI.
threat_scenarios.execution_support_note: The emulator supports service enumeration and interface introspection through its D-Bus-compatible control surface.
threat_scenarios.cybersecurity_goal: An unauthenticated session should not be able to obtain an inventory of exposed services, interfaces, and functions.
threat_scenarios.precondition: The attacker has already obtained an unauthenticated IVI control session.

T1.description: The attacker collects the list of exposed services.

T2.description: The attacker identifies callable interfaces exposed by those services.

T3.description: The attacker distinguishes read-oriented functions from state-changing functions.

attack_paths.path_description: A remote attacker enumerates the exposed IVI service surface

damage_scenarios.description: IVI function-abuse scenarios derived from the Jeep Cherokee whitepaper

threat_scenarios.title: A remote attacker obtains vehicle location and identifiers
threat_scenarios.attack_vector: Remote network via IVI control session
threat_scenarios.logical_path_summary: Unauthenticated IVI control session -> IVI location-query function
threat_scenarios.threat_scenario: A remote attacker uses an unauthenticated IVI control session to obtain vehicle location information and vehicle identifiers.
threat_scenarios.execution_support_note: The emulator exposes a location-query function and returns structured location and vehicle-identification data.
threat_scenarios.cybersecurity_goal: Location and vehicle-identifying information shall not be provided to unauthenticated sessions.
threat_scenarios.precondition: The attacker has already obtained an unauthenticated IVI control session.

T1.description: The attacker identifies the location-query function.

T2.description: The attacker invokes the location-query function without authentication.

T3.description: The attacker collects the returned location and vehicle-identification information.

attack_paths.path_description: A remote attacker obtains vehicle location and identifiers

threat_scenarios.title: A remote attacker performs unauthorized HVAC control
threat_scenarios.attack_vector: Remote network via IVI control session
threat_scenarios.logical_path_summary: Unauthenticated IVI control session -> IVI HVAC control function
threat_scenarios.threat_scenario: A remote attacker uses an unauthenticated IVI control session to change HVAC settings without authorization.
threat_scenarios.execution_support_note: The emulator exposes a state-changing HVAC function together with a query path that can validate the resulting state.
threat_scenarios.cybersecurity_goal: The HVAC control function shall not be modifiable by an unauthenticated session.
threat_scenarios.precondition: The attacker has already obtained an unauthenticated IVI control session.

T1.description: The attacker identifies the HVAC control function.

T2.description: The attacker submits the desired cabin-environment settings.

T3.description: The attacker verifies that the HVAC state changed.

attack_paths.path_description: A remote attacker performs unauthorized HVAC control

threat_scenarios.title: A remote attacker changes radio volume without authorization
threat_scenarios.attack_vector: Remote network via IVI control session
threat_scenarios.logical_path_summary: Unauthenticated IVI control session -> IVI audio-output control function
threat_scenarios.threat_scenario: A remote attacker uses an unauthenticated IVI control session to change the radio output volume without authorization.
threat_scenarios.execution_support_note: The emulator exposes an audio-output control function and a state query that can verify a volume change.
threat_scenarios.cybersecurity_goal: The audio-output control function shall not be modifiable by an unauthenticated session.
threat_scenarios.precondition: The attacker has already obtained an unauthenticated IVI control session.

T1.description: The attacker identifies the audio-output control function.

T2.description: The attacker requests a change to the target volume state.

T3.description: The attacker verifies that the volume state changed.

attack_paths.path_description: A remote attacker changes radio volume without authorization

threat_scenarios.title: A remote attacker changes the radio channel without authorization
threat_scenarios.attack_vector: Remote network via IVI control session
threat_scenarios.logical_path_summary: Unauthenticated IVI control session -> IVI radio-tuner function
threat_scenarios.threat_scenario: A remote attacker uses an unauthenticated IVI control session to change the selected radio channel without authorization.
threat_scenarios.execution_support_note: The emulator exposes a radio tuning function and a state query that can verify a channel change.
threat_scenarios.cybersecurity_goal: The radio-tuner function shall not be modifiable by an unauthenticated session.
threat_scenarios.precondition: The attacker has already obtained an unauthenticated IVI control session.

T1.description: The attacker identifies the radio-tuner function.

T2.description: The attacker requests a change to the target frequency or channel.

T3.description: The attacker verifies that the selected broadcast channel changed.

attack_paths.path_description: A remote attacker changes the radio channel without authorization

threat_scenarios.title: A remote attacker changes the display-output state without authorization
threat_scenarios.attack_vector: Remote network via IVI control session
threat_scenarios.logical_path_summary: Unauthenticated IVI control session -> IVI display-output control function
threat_scenarios.threat_scenario: A remote attacker uses an unauthenticated IVI control session to change the display-output state without authorization.
threat_scenarios.execution_support_note: The emulator exposes a display-state change function that can be observed through state changes and logs.
threat_scenarios.cybersecurity_goal: The display-output control function shall not be modifiable by an unauthenticated session.
threat_scenarios.precondition: The attacker has already obtained an unauthenticated IVI control session.

T1.description: The attacker identifies the display-output control function.

T2.description: The attacker invokes a control that changes the display state.

T3.description: The attacker confirms that a display-state change occurs.

attack_paths.path_description: A remote attacker changes the display-output state without authorization

damage_scenarios.description: In-vehicle bus observation and message-injection scenarios aligned to the Jeep Cherokee whitepaper and the current emulator

threat_scenarios.title: An attacker captures in-vehicle bus traffic through an IVI-reachable CAN bridge
threat_scenarios.entry_point: CAN bridge exposed by the IVI emulator
threat_scenarios.logical_path_summary: IVI-reachable CAN bridge -> bus traffic observation stream
threat_scenarios.threat_scenario: An attacker gains access to an IVI-reachable CAN bridge and passively observes bus traffic to identify message families associated with targeted vehicle functions.
threat_scenarios.execution_support_note: The emulator's CAN bridge can expose continuous background traffic and can be configured to block or allow IVI-to-CAN access.
threat_scenarios.cybersecurity_goal: Traffic on internal vehicle networks must not be observable from the IVI domain without authorization.
threat_scenarios.precondition: A CAN bridge reachable from the IVI domain exists.

T1.description: The attacker connects to the CAN bridge interface.

T2.description: The attacker opens a bus channel for traffic observation.

T3.description: The attacker enters a passive traffic-observation mode.

T4.description: The attacker identifies message families associated with targeted vehicle functions.

attack_paths.path_description: An attacker captures in-vehicle bus traffic through an IVI-reachable CAN bridge

threat_scenarios.title: An attacker injects a forged braking-control message on the safety-critical vehicle bus
threat_scenarios.attack_vector: Safety-critical vehicle bus via IVI-reachable bridge
threat_scenarios.entry_point: CAN bridge exposed by the IVI emulator
threat_scenarios.endpoint: Braking-control function on the safety-critical vehicle bus
threat_scenarios.logical_path_summary: IVI-reachable CAN bridge -> braking-control function on the safety-critical vehicle bus
threat_scenarios.whitepaper_section: Cyber Physical CAN messages – No brakes
threat_scenarios.threat_scenario: An attacker injects a forged safety-critical vehicle-bus message that targets the braking-control function and causes the braking behavior to be altered or disabled.
threat_scenarios.execution_support_note: The emulator accepts injected bus frames for the braking controller path when domain isolation is disabled and message authentication is not enforced.
threat_scenarios.cybersecurity_goal: The braking function must not be influenced by unauthenticated vehicle-bus messages.
threat_scenarios.precondition: The attacker has access to the IVI-reachable CAN bridge and an open bus channel.

T1.description: The attacker identifies a bus message pattern associated with the braking-control function.

T2.description: The attacker crafts a forged braking-control message.

T3.description: The attacker injects the forged message through the CAN bridge.

T4.description: The attacker verifies whether the braking-control function accepted the injected message.

attack_paths.path_description: An attacker injects a forged braking-control message on the safety-critical vehicle bus

threat_scenarios.title: An attacker injects a forged steering-control message on the safety-critical vehicle bus
threat_scenarios.attack_vector: Safety-critical vehicle bus via IVI-reachable bridge
threat_scenarios.entry_point: CAN bridge exposed by the IVI emulator
threat_scenarios.endpoint: Steering-control function on the safety-critical vehicle bus
threat_scenarios.logical_path_summary: IVI-reachable CAN bridge -> steering-control function on the safety-critical vehicle bus
threat_scenarios.threat_scenario: An attacker injects a forged safety-critical vehicle-bus message that targets the steering-control function and influences electric power steering behavior.
threat_scenarios.execution_support_note: The emulator accepts injected bus frames for the steering-control path when domain isolation is disabled and message authentication is not enforced.
threat_scenarios.cybersecurity_goal: The steering function must not be influenced by unauthenticated vehicle-bus messages.
threat_scenarios.precondition: The attacker has access to the IVI-reachable CAN bridge and an open bus channel.

T1.description: The attacker identifies a bus message pattern associated with the steering-control function.

T2.description: The attacker crafts a forged steering-control message.

T3.description: The attacker injects the forged message through the CAN bridge.

T4.description: The attacker verifies whether the steering-control function accepted the injected message.

attack_paths.path_description: An attacker injects a forged steering-control message on the safety-critical vehicle bus

threat_scenarios.title: An attacker injects a forged powertrain-control message to stop the engine
threat_scenarios.attack_vector: Safety-critical vehicle bus via IVI-reachable bridge
threat_scenarios.entry_point: CAN bridge exposed by the IVI emulator
threat_scenarios.endpoint: Powertrain and engine-control function on the safety-critical vehicle bus
threat_scenarios.logical_path_summary: IVI-reachable CAN bridge -> powertrain and engine-control function on the safety-critical vehicle bus
threat_scenarios.whitepaper_section: Cyber Physical CAN messages – Kill engine
threat_scenarios.threat_scenario: An attacker injects a forged safety-critical vehicle-bus message that targets the powertrain-control function and causes the engine to stop or lose propulsion.
threat_scenarios.execution_support_note: The emulator accepts injected bus frames for the engine-control path when domain isolation is disabled and message authentication is not enforced.
threat_scenarios.cybersecurity_goal: The engine must not be stoppable through unauthenticated vehicle-bus messages.
threat_scenarios.precondition: The attacker has access to the IVI-reachable CAN bridge and an open bus channel.

T1.description: The attacker identifies a bus message pattern associated with the powertrain-control function.

T2.description: The attacker crafts a forged powertrain-control message intended to stop or disrupt engine operation.

T3.description: The attacker injects the forged message through the CAN bridge.

T4.description: The attacker verifies whether the powertrain-control function accepted the injected message.

attack_paths.path_description: An attacker injects a forged powertrain-control message to stop the engine

threat_scenarios.title: An attacker injects a forged transmission-control message to disable the transmission
threat_scenarios.attack_vector: Safety-critical vehicle bus via IVI-reachable bridge
threat_scenarios.entry_point: CAN bridge exposed by the IVI emulator
threat_scenarios.endpoint: Transmission-control function on the safety-critical vehicle bus
threat_scenarios.logical_path_summary: IVI-reachable CAN bridge -> transmission-control function on the safety-critical vehicle bus
threat_scenarios.whitepaper_section: Cyber Physical CAN messages – Transmission disruption
threat_scenarios.threat_scenario: An attacker injects a forged safety-critical vehicle-bus message that targets the transmission-control function and disrupts transmission behavior.
threat_scenarios.execution_support_note: The emulator accepts injected bus frames for the transmission-control path when domain isolation is disabled and message authentication is not enforced.
threat_scenarios.cybersecurity_goal: The transmission must not be controllable through unauthenticated vehicle-bus messages.
threat_scenarios.precondition: The attacker has access to the IVI-reachable CAN bridge and an open bus channel.

T1.description: The attacker identifies a bus message pattern associated with the transmission-control function.

T2.description: The attacker crafts a forged transmission-control message.

T3.description: The attacker injects the forged message through the CAN bridge.

T4.description: The attacker verifies whether the transmission-control function accepted the injected message.

attack_paths.path_description: An attacker injects a forged transmission-control message to disable the transmission

threat_scenarios.title: An attacker injects a forged comfort-bus message to alter the climate-control setting
threat_scenarios.attack_vector: Comfort vehicle bus via IVI-reachable bridge
threat_scenarios.entry_point: CAN bridge exposed by the IVI emulator
threat_scenarios.endpoint: Climate-control function on the comfort vehicle bus
threat_scenarios.logical_path_summary: IVI-reachable CAN bridge -> climate-control function on the comfort vehicle bus
threat_scenarios.threat_scenario: An attacker injects a forged comfort-bus message that targets the climate-control function and alters the cabin climate setting.
threat_scenarios.execution_support_note: The emulator accepts injected bus frames for the comfort climate-control path when domain isolation is disabled and message authentication is not enforced.
threat_scenarios.cybersecurity_goal: The climate-control function must not be modified through unauthenticated vehicle-bus messages.
threat_scenarios.precondition: The attacker has access to the IVI-reachable CAN bridge and an open bus channel.

T1.description: The attacker identifies a bus message pattern associated with the climate-control function.

T2.description: The attacker crafts a forged comfort-bus climate-control message.

T3.description: The attacker injects the forged message through the CAN bridge.

T4.description: The attacker verifies whether the climate-control state changed.

attack_paths.path_description: An attacker injects a forged comfort-bus message to alter the climate-control setting

threat_scenarios.title: An attacker injects a forged comfort-bus message to alter audio amplifier behavior
threat_scenarios.attack_vector: Comfort vehicle bus via IVI-reachable bridge
threat_scenarios.entry_point: CAN bridge exposed by the IVI emulator
threat_scenarios.endpoint: Audio-output function on the comfort vehicle bus
threat_scenarios.logical_path_summary: IVI-reachable CAN bridge -> audio-output function on the comfort vehicle bus
threat_scenarios.threat_scenario: An attacker injects a forged comfort-bus message that targets the audio-output function and alters amplifier or audio-output behavior.
threat_scenarios.execution_support_note: The emulator accepts injected bus frames for the comfort audio-output path when domain isolation is disabled and message authentication is not enforced.
threat_scenarios.cybersecurity_goal: The audio-output function must not be modified through unauthenticated vehicle-bus messages.
threat_scenarios.precondition: The attacker has access to the IVI-reachable CAN bridge and an open bus channel.

T1.description: The attacker identifies a bus message pattern associated with the audio-output function.

T2.description: The attacker crafts a forged comfort-bus audio-control message.

T3.description: The attacker injects the forged message through the CAN bridge.

T4.description: The attacker verifies whether the audio-output state changed.

attack_paths.path_description: An attacker injects a forged comfort-bus message to alter audio amplifier behavior

damage_scenarios.description: OTA firmware update tampering scenarios aligned to the Jeep Cherokee whitepaper and the current emulator

threat_scenarios.title: An attacker submits an unsigned firmware package to the OTA update service
threat_scenarios.logical_path_summary: OTA firmware update endpoint -> firmware signature-verification function
threat_scenarios.whitepaper_section: Updating the V850 / Flashing the V850 without USB
threat_scenarios.threat_scenario: An attacker submits a firmware update package without a valid signature to the OTA update service and verifies whether the package is accepted.
threat_scenarios.execution_support_note: The emulator's OTA service can accept or reject firmware packages based on a signature-verification flag.
threat_scenarios.cybersecurity_goal: Firmware packages must not be accepted without valid cryptographic signature verification.
threat_scenarios.precondition: The OTA firmware update endpoint is reachable.

T1.description: The attacker identifies the OTA firmware update endpoint.

T2.description: The attacker requests update-related information from the firmware service.

T3.description: The attacker submits a firmware package without a valid signature.

T4.description: The attacker verifies whether the unsigned package was accepted.

attack_paths.path_description: An attacker submits an unsigned firmware package to the OTA update service

threat_scenarios.title: An attacker uses the OTA firmware service over an unprotected channel
threat_scenarios.entry_point: OTA firmware update endpoint without transport protection
threat_scenarios.endpoint: Firmware update service over an unprotected channel
threat_scenarios.logical_path_summary: OTA firmware update endpoint without transport protection -> firmware update service
threat_scenarios.threat_scenario: An attacker connects to the OTA firmware service over an unprotected channel and verifies that firmware operations remain possible without transport-layer protection.
threat_scenarios.execution_support_note: The emulator can expose the firmware update service over a protected or unprotected transport depending on the TLS-enforcement flag.
threat_scenarios.cybersecurity_goal: OTA firmware communication must be protected by transport-layer encryption.
threat_scenarios.precondition: The OTA service is reachable while transport protection is disabled.

T1.description: The attacker attempts to connect to the OTA firmware service over an unprotected channel.

T2.description: The attacker verifies that the connection succeeds without transport-layer protection.

T3.description: The attacker confirms that firmware-related operations remain possible over the unprotected channel.

attack_paths.path_description: An attacker uses the OTA firmware service over an unprotected channel

damage_scenarios.description: Remote diagnostic-protocol abuse scenario supported by the current emulator

threat_scenarios.title: An attacker bypasses diagnostic access control and triggers an ECU reset over a remote diagnostic gateway
threat_scenarios.logical_path_summary: Remote diagnostic gateway -> diagnostic access-control bypass -> ECU-reset function
threat_scenarios.whitepaper_section: Extension scenario – emulator-supported diagnostic protocol abuse
threat_scenarios.threat_scenario: An attacker connects to a remote diagnostic gateway, bypasses diagnostic access control, and invokes an ECU reset without satisfying the intended authentication path.
threat_scenarios.execution_support_note: The emulator includes a DoIP/UDS service that can toggle diagnostic authentication enforcement and ECU-reset protection.
threat_scenarios.cybersecurity_goal: Diagnostic services must enforce access control before safety-relevant operations such as ECU reset are executed.
threat_scenarios.precondition: The remote diagnostic gateway is reachable.

T1.description: The attacker connects to the remote diagnostic gateway.

T2.description: The attacker requests a diagnostic session without first satisfying the intended authentication process.

T3.description: The attacker probes the diagnostic access-control mechanism to determine whether it can be bypassed.

T4.description: The attacker attempts a reset operation through the diagnostic service.

T5.description: The attacker verifies whether the reset operation was executed.

attack_paths.path_description: An attacker bypasses diagnostic access control and triggers an ECU reset over a remote diagnostic gateway

damage_scenarios.description: Service-oriented communication abuse scenario supported by the current emulator

threat_scenarios.title: An attacker invokes service-oriented vehicle functions without authentication
threat_scenarios.logical_path_summary: Service-oriented vehicle service endpoint -> readable and state-changing vehicle functions without authentication
threat_scenarios.whitepaper_section: Extension scenario – emulator-supported service-oriented communication abuse
threat_scenarios.threat_scenario: An attacker connects to a service-oriented vehicle endpoint and invokes readable and state-changing functions without satisfying any authentication requirement.
threat_scenarios.execution_support_note: The emulator includes a service-oriented vehicle endpoint that can allow or deny read and write method calls depending on the authentication flag.
threat_scenarios.cybersecurity_goal: Service-oriented vehicle functions must enforce authentication before data disclosure or state modification.
threat_scenarios.precondition: The service-oriented vehicle endpoint is reachable.

T1.description: The attacker connects to the service-oriented vehicle endpoint.

T2.description: The attacker invokes a readable vehicle function without authentication.

T3.description: The attacker verifies whether sensitive data is returned or access is denied.

T4.description: The attacker invokes a state-changing vehicle function without authentication.

T5.description: The attacker verifies whether the vehicle state was modified.

attack_paths.path_description: An attacker invokes service-oriented vehicle functions without authentication

review_notes: Preserved the 19-scenario set from the operator's expanded implemented file.
Normalized preconditions into a single precondition string per scenario for pipeline_v4 compatibility.
Converted the trees to generalized-tree form with explicit attack_paths so Step 1, Step 3-1, Step 4A, and Step 5 can preserve path order and path-level controls.
Added scenario-level selected_security_requirements and per-path cc_sfr/nist_sp_800_53 so current path-control binding logic has authoritative inputs.

Reduced exact binding leakage in titles and attack-step descriptions by removing direct API signatures, port literals, arbitration IDs, and service IDs from the threat text while preserving the same scenario inventory and CONN_x bindings.
Retained whitepaper_fidelity tags to distinguish direct whitepaper scenarios, surrogate emulator realizations, and emulator-specific extension scenarios.
